filesystem.name: filesystem
filesystem.description: MCP server for file operations. Allows AI to save code examples, notes, and other content to user-specific directories.

brave-search.name: brave-search
brave-search.description: MCP server for web search using Brave Search API. Enables AI to search for current information and up-to-date examples.

playwright.name: playwright
playwright.description: MCP server for browser automation using Playwright. Allows AI to open web pages, take screenshots, interact with elements, and analyze page layout. More modern and stable than Puppeteer.

documentation.overview: Model Context Protocol (MCP) configuration for AI Study Agent. MCP servers extend AI capabilities by providing tools for file operations, web search, browser automation, and more.

BRAVE_API_KEY.description: API key for Brave Search. Required for web search functionality.
BRAVE_API_KEY.howToGet: Sign up at https://brave.com/search/api/ to get your API key

MCP_ENABLED.description: Enable or disable MCP integration globally

MCP_FILESYSTEM_PATH.description: Base path for user file storage

filesystem.purpose: Provides file system operations for saving and managing user files
tools: read_file - Read content from a file
write_file - Write content to a file
list_directory - List files in a directory
useCases: Save code examples for students to download
security: All file paths are validated to prevent directory traversal
Files are isolated in user-specific directories

brave-search.purpose: Enables web search for current information and up-to-date content
tools: brave_web_search - Search the web using Brave Search API
useCases: Find current information about programming topics
Discover recent examples and best practices
features: Results are cached for 1 hour to reduce API calls
Limited to top 5 most relevant results

playwright.purpose: Enables browser automation for visual testing and page interaction
tools: playwright_screenshot - Take a screenshot of the page
playwright_fill - Fill in a form field
playwright_select - Select an option from a dropdown
playwright_evaluate - Execute JavaScript in the page context
playwright_get_visible_text - Get visible text content from the page
playwright_get_visible_html - Get HTML content from the page
useCases: Visual regression testing of UI components
Analyze page layout and styling issues
Test responsive design across different viewports
Verify button positions and element alignment
features: Runs in headless Chromium browser (supports Firefox and WebKit too)
More stable and modern than Puppeteer

steps: 1. Install required MCP server packages (handled automatically by npx)
2. Set up environment variables in .env file
3. Configure server settings in this file if needed
4. Restart the application to apply changes
verification: Check application logs for '[MCP] Initialization complete'
Visit settings page to see server status indicators
Test file save functionality in AI chat
Test web search functionality in AI chat
Test browser automation by asking AI to open a page and take a screenshot

troubleshooting.serverNotRunning: Check that required environment variables are set and npx can access the MCP server packages
troubleshooting.toolCallFailed: Review server logs for specific error messages. Common issues: missing API keys, network connectivity, invalid arguments
troubleshooting.filesystemPermissions: Ensure the application has write permissions to the configured filesystem path
troubleshooting.searchRateLimit: Brave Search API has rate limits. Results are cached to minimize API calls.
troubleshooting.playwrightBrowserIssues: Playwright downloads Chromium automatically. Ensure you have sufficient disk space and internet connectivity. Playwright is more stable and actively maintained than Puppeteer.
